nodes.context: Criminal organization consisting of 6 crime families, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and murder.
One of the 6 crime families of the Puerto Rican Mob, founded by Quitoni Martinez.
Colombian drug cartel that had connections with the Puerto Rican Mob.
Colombian paramilitary group that had small connections with the Puerto Rican Mob.
Criminal organization that had small connections with the Puerto Rican Mob.
Mexican drug cartel that had connections and later conflict with the Puerto Rican Mob.
Puerto Rican street gang that had connections with the Puerto Rican Mob.
Italian-American organized crime group allied with the Puerto Rican Mob.
Mexican drug cartel allied with the Puerto Rican Mob (defunct).
Street gang allied with the Puerto Rican Mob.
Russian organized crime group allied with the Puerto Rican Mob.
Criminal organization allied with the Puerto Rican Mob.
Rival criminal organization to the Puerto Rican Mob.
Rival criminal organization to the Puerto Rican Mob.
Rival criminal organization to the Puerto Rican Mob (defunct).
Rival drug cartel to the Puerto Rican Mob (defunct).
Rival criminal organization to the Puerto Rican Mob.
One of six crime families in the Puerto Rican Mafia, operating in northwestern Puerto Rico.
Colombian drug cartel with strong connections to the Estrella Family.
Colombian paramilitary group with small connections to the Estrella Family.
Criminal organization with small connections to the Estrella Family.
Mexican drug cartel with small connections to the Estrella Family.
Puerto Rican street gang with small connections to the Estrella Family.
A family within the Puerto Rican Mafia, headed by Don Gonzalez.

edges.evidence_quote: They soon began having issues with the Mexican cartel known as Gulf Cartel who asked for $35 million for their help in making deals in Mexico. The Gulf Cartel soon started blackmailing the Puerto Rican mafia, which resulted in the kidnapping and killing of several members of the Gulf Cartel.
edges.context: The Puerto Rican Mob had conflict with the Gulf Cartel over money and blackmail, leading to kidnappings and killings.
